When the safari goes in the jungle for a ride, if it detects sound, it takes a short sample and tries to classify it according to a pre-trained prediction model. The machine learning algorithm used was random forest. We were successfully able to train the model on lion , tiger, peocock, wolf , elephant and several more such wild animals. The sound samples were obtained from several animal sound repositories. The model achieved an accuracy of around 87% on the test data surmounting problems like noise in sound files, lack of extensive training examples. We also used a Wi-fi module so that information about the animal detected can be broadcast to the tourists' mobile devices.
